# How long should stories be?

That's up to you! Some questions may spark long stories, while others may only need a few sentences to answer. Just write or say what you remember—even the simplest memories can mean so much.

# What if it takes longer than a year to finish?

That’s totally fine! You’ll have access to write, edit, and order copies of your book, even after your subscription year has ended. Weekly questions are there to provide helpful structure, but this is your story to tell at your pace.

# What are your audio recording options?

There are three ways to tell stories over a phone call - even on a landline! We can transcribe them word for word, or you can talk with Storyworth or a family member, and we’ll transform your conversations into stories. No apps or passwords required.

# What are your questions like?

We have the largest library of curated questions covering everything from childhood and education to parenthood, travel, and hobbies. You can also write your own questions or share a few life details to get personalized questions.

# What happens to my stories at the end of the year?

We’re committed to preserving your stories forever. They’ll always be private, owned by you, and available to read and edit. You can also download a free **audiobook or** PDF copy of your memoir anytime.

# What are your writing options?

You can always write stories by either replying to your weekly email prompt or on the Storyworth site. You can add photos or edit your stories at any time.

WHAT IS REMENTO

## A book of your loved one's life stories, told in their voice.

Remento turns a year of weekly spoken memories into a hardcover book of beautifully written stories. Every chapter has a QR code that plays back the original recording, so you can read both the story your loved one told, and hear them tell it.

HOW IT WORKS

## Stories on the page. Voice just a scan away.

![](https://www.remento.co/cdn/webflow/62d8110cd6b0954a89c7d41c/6a102783cfd971407fa90926_img-01.avif)

step 1

### Pick the questions. Make them personal.

Choose from hundreds of prompts designed to surface stories your family never heard. Or upload photos to learn the story behind each one.

![](https://www.remento.co/cdn/webflow/62d8110cd6b0954a89c7d41c/6a102786bd794d5bca7fc5b2_img-02.avif)

step 2

### A question arrives. They just speak.

Every week, Remento sends them a prompt by email or text. No app. No login. No password. Two clicks and they're recording on any device.

![](https://www.remento.co/cdn/webflow/62d8110cd6b0954a89c7d41c/6a102787bd794d5bca7fc614_img-03.avif)

step 3

### Their words become a written story.

Speech-to-Story™ turns each recording into a polished written chapter. Word-for-word transcript or flowing narrative. Fully editable.

![](https://www.remento.co/cdn/webflow/62d8110cd6b0954a89c7d41c/6a102787a058e524234375e5_img-04.avif)

step 4

### Your family hears it the moment it's done.

Every new story is instantly shared with the family. Read it, listen to it, react to it. For a lot of families, this becomes the best part of the week.

![Photo of a man holding a phone. He looks like he is telling a story](https://www.remento.co/cdn/webflow/62d8110cd6b0954a89c7d41c/6a1fddb6d568a0cc45c453af_img.avif)

Our Story

In 2017, my mom was diagnosed with cancer, and I suddenly felt an urgent need to preserve her voice. I bought her a memoir-writing book, but she never used it. “Writing feels like homework,” she said.

So I pulled out my phone, hit record, and we just talked. I learned more than I ever could have imagined.

That’s why we built Remento, the simplest way to preserve a parent's memories for the future. Because one day, having those stories and their voice will be more precious than you could ever know.

From our family to yours, happy storytelling.

## Frequently Asked Questions

What’s included in my purchase?

Our standard package includes a year’s worth of unlimited story prompts for one storyteller, unlimited collaborators, and one credit for a color-printed hardcover book (up to 380 pages). Shipping in the United States is included in the purchase price, and [for a fee outside the US](https://help.remento.co/en/articles/8365901-shipping-destinations-and-fees). You can buy additional copies of your book for $69 each.

We also offer a 30-day money-back guarantee if you're not completely satisfied.

Why is there a subscription, and what happens after the first year?

Your $99 purchase includes a full year of storytelling and one hardcover book. This model gives you/your storyteller ample time to collect meaningful stories without rushing.

But don’t worry, you’ll never be locked into a subscription.

After a year, you can either renew or cancel your subscription. By renewing for either $99/year or $12/month, you'll be able to continue recording new stories and creating new books. If you don't renew, you can still view all of your family's recordings and download any recordings or photographs from our website at any time.

Can I edit the story that gets written?

Yes, you can edit every story before it’s printed.

For each response, you can choose how it appears. Keep it as a cleaned transcript of your exact words, or turn it into a more polished story in either first person or third person. You can switch between versions anytime, adjust the length, and edit the text directly.

The original recording is always preserved, so you keep both the final story and the voice exactly as it was told.

What if Remento goes out of business?

That’s certainly not our plan! And, this content is far too precious to take any chances with. That's why we've designed Remento with permanence in mind.

All your content is yours to download at any time, including all original recordings and transcripts. That way you can back up this content to your favorite device (e.g., computer, tablet, smartphone) or cloud storage account (e.g., iCloud, Google Drive). Plus, the physical book itself is a permanent keepsake that will last for generations regardless of our company's future.

Is Remento easy enough for my grandparents?

Yes! We designed Remento specifically with older adults in mind. There are no apps to download, no passwords to remember, and no complicated technology to learn. Your storyteller simply clicks a link in their email or text message and records their response.

We've had storytellers in their 90s use Remento with ease ([like Gerald](https://www.remento.co/journal/remento-book-review-gerald-p)). If your loved one can answer a phone call or click an email link, they can use Remento. And if they ever need help, our support team is just a message away.

# Apps and Services for Life Stories

[Storytelling](/tips/category/Storytelling)[Family History](/tips/category/Family%2BHistory)[Your Stories](/tips/category/Your%2BStories)[Their Memories](/tips/category/Their%2BMemories)

May 17

Written By [Barbara Campbell](/?author=6365eabcc01b1722c1a0fef1)

![person holds iphone to view app icons near laptop on desk](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/65b5d98a6d9799259dad7360/dc688f80-d1ad-4028-b473-fcc02fac8ccb/apps-mobile-laptop.jpg)

Streamline your life stories project with apps and services for genealogy, voice-recording, self-publishing, and more! /*Photo by Wdnet via Pexels*

## Apps, learning resources and services are available to help you capture and share life stories – as you research, write, record audio, arrange visuals – and get your project done!

There are many apps (user-friendly software applications), resources and services available to help with your life stories project. Which ones interest you will depend on [which format you choose](/tips/ways-to-tell-life-stories) (such as written, audio or visual) for a life story.

Here are the main kinds of resources and some popular examples. I’ve used a number of these, but not all. I looked into them and some reviews, to help you be aware of options.

> “Is there an app for that?”

— (probably yes, and people too!)

### 1) User-Friendly Tech to Capture Memories

Some apps to help capture life stories. Of course, social media apps such as *Facebook* and *Instagram* help you share some of your life’s moments.

#### Voice Record on a Smart Phone

If you have a smart phone, you can record your voice as you tell one of your stories, make audio-notes of some of your memories, or record a life-stories interview with someone.

#### Collect and Share Memories

Apps and online services are available to help record your life stories and [capture memories of loved ones](/tips/benefits-of-preserving-memories), such as:

*Storyworth* – sends a question a week for someone to reply by email, then provides a keepsake book with photos at the end of the year.

*Audiobiography* – sends weekly questions for someone to voice-record their answers. Friends and family can listen to these like a podcast. You can buy a book to go with the recordings.

*RecordMeNow* – a free app that helps someone record key things about themselves, e.g. for a parent to leave a legacy for their child. The person records video interviews in answer to question prompts.

#### Family History Story Apps

Ancestry and genealogy websites offer user-friendly ways to capture and share your family stories and memories.

*FamilySearch* – (a free website for all) has their *Memories* app for ancestor photos and stories, and *Record My Story* for audio files of your stories and stories of your family.

*Ancestry’s Storymaker Studio* – lets you enhance photos, capture family stories you narrate into an audio file, plus share photos and stories.

*MyHeritage Photo Storyteller* – (a mobile app) allows you to record yourself or interview family members to tell the story behind any of your family photos.

#### Online Memorials

A newspaper obituary is a good way to reach people locally in print, as well as view online.

An online memorial page provides for more space, numerous photos and a virtual guestbook. Plus, you can update it if you wish.

* *WeRemember.com* – a free online memorial space where you can write or upload an obituary, friends and family can add their photos and memories about the person, plus comment and share their stories
* *EverLoved.com* – a free online service that creates a custom website about your loved one (with its own unique link), including the obituary you submit, photos, and more
* Local funeral homes may post an online obituary as part of their service. If you’d like broader reach (via a large well-known website), there are paid-for options such as *Legacy.com* and *Tributes Archive*.

### 2) Digital Images for Stories

These photo applications work for life storytelling as well as other projects.

#### Photo Enhancing

There is a wide range of photo software programs, for sure. *Windows* includes its photo program for PC, as does *Apple* for Mac computers. Each type of smart phone enables you to take and edit photos.

* *Vivid-Pix* and *MyHeritage Re-Imagine* – enable you to scan, sharpen and colorize black and white photos.
* *My Heritage DeepNostalgia* – uses AI to simulate movement (facial expressions) from a photograph in a brief animation. *DeepStory* adds a voice to an animated image, as if your ancestor is telling a story of your family history.

![pile of old printed family photos with parents and children](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/65b5d98a6d9799259dad7360/33337518-53ac-4f2f-b7b5-69090ad7fb31/old-family-photos.jpg)

User-friendly technology can help you feature photos in your life storytelling. / *Photo by Rodolfo Clix via Pexels*

#### Digital Scrapbooks

Digital scrapbooking software is available for free or to buy. Popular ones include *MyMemories* and *MixBook*.

#### Slideshows and Movies

*Windows Movie Maker* comes with a PC, and *Apple iMovies* comes with a new Mac or mobile device such as iPhone. Any smart phone enables you to take videos, and create slideshows from still photos.

The sky is the limit for digital cameras and movie-making software. So, use what you have, or check out what fits with your aims, your budget, and where you plan to show your work.

#### Custom-Made Maps

*Google Maps* has their *GoogleMyMaps* function where you can create and save customized maps for free, as long as they’re not for commercial use. The same goes for *Google Earth* and *Google Earth Pro*. You could start with one app and progress to the next, as you learn more features.

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/65b5d98a6d9799259dad7360/ae573e2f-c392-4288-a22a-f9e2b5cfb576/digital-map-on-computer.jpg)

Easy-to-create maps let you share family history and tell your own life stories! / *Photo by Yucel Moran via Unsplash*

#### Custom-Made Calendars

*Google Calendar* is the most common one online. You can set up calendars to share with family (or any person or group) to tell a bit about key dates in your life, your family or group, and/or your ancestors.

Photo shops and other print services enable you to custom-make and print calendars to give to family and friends. *Shutterfly* and *MixBook* are popular online options.

### 3) Writing and Storytelling

There are many DIY (Do-It-Yourself) resources to help with your writing and life-stories projects.

#### Workbooks and Resources

There are many workbooks, how-to guides, audio books and other resources available to help you develop your writing skills and complete your life-stories project. You’re best to search online – and in your local library and bookstores – for resources that fit your needs and skill level.

Resources are geared toward specific types of writing, such as: short stories, memoir, biography, or family history. Over time, you could work with different formats and approaches.

Most likely, the ones that will help you most to tell stories from your life will be for ‘creative writing’. Although they are geared toward fiction, you can use the methods for true-life stories.

#### Courses and Workshops

Many writers and schools offer workshops and courses at different levels. There are specialty courses for short stories, memoir, family history, and other types of life-story writing.

![students with notebooks listen to teacher while sitting around library table](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/65b5d98a6d9799259dad7360/961bea51-ed5b-4e01-ac08-aa77ca9f0b4f/writing-workshop-class.jpg)

Beyond blogs and DIY books – workshops provide interactive learning and support. / *Photo by Kampus Production via Pexels*

You can take classes online, attend local workshops in-person, or go on a writing-retreat course.

Look to find the type and timing you prefer, within your budget.

#### AI for Life Stories

AI technology for writing (and research, and image creation, and more) is now publicly available. Many apps are free, others have a fee to use or access more features.

Platforms and services include:

*ChatGPT* – for any kind of life stories, poems, essays, image creation and more

*MyHeritage* *AI Biographer* – a tool for ancestor stories

*Storian* – you record your stories aloud, their AI writes, and they help you edit into a book

---

*Personally, I’m not ready to embrace this as a substitute for writing. Yet I’d like you to be aware of options. Even with concerns about accuracy, quality and copyright, AI has much to offer. Along with potential problems come possibilities for life storytelling.*

---

#### Story Professionals

If it fits for you, you might like to hire a professional to help with your life stories project. Here are some main types.

* **story coach** – a story coach tends to help with fiction, but can help you with your real-life (non-fiction) stories. They help with story structure, character, and narrative flow. They’re also like a personal trainer for you and your writing project.
* **life story writer** – sometimes called a personal historian – asks you questions, listens to your stories, takes notes and recordings, then writes your story for you. They’ll offer to prepare it as a book for you to self-publish, with photos you choose to include.
* **oral historian, videographer** – these professionals are like life-story writers, yet their media is audio and visual. They record interviews with you or your loved one, then edit the material into a set of voice or video recordings to tell your story overall.

Find someone that suits you, to work with in-person or meet virtually.

### 4) Researching Your Ancestry

Here are some key resources to help you [discover your family history](/tips/discover-your-family-history).

#### Genealogy Websites

The largest websites worldwide to help you discover, organize and share your family history are (in alphabetical order):

* *Ancestry*
* *FamilySearch*
* *FindMyPast*
* *MyHeritage*

Together, they are known as ‘The Big Four’.

Family Search is free of charge. The others offer a basic level for free, with upgrades (to access more features) for a subscription fee.

Each of these provides an overall service, and has their own specialties. You can choose what suits you best over time, or sign up for more than one.

---

*Personally, Ancestry is where I have my main family tree, and do initial research and sharing. American Ancestors is great for detailed research and courses about my colonial ancestors. I’ve much more yet to discover on MyHeritage, including my DNA matches. FindMyPast will help me connect with local family history societies in Scotland (once I know where). Family Search helps with research and how-to resources.*

---

Some sites have a geographic or ethnic focus, such as: *Geneanet* (Continental Europe) and *JewishGen* (Jewish genealogy).

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/65b5d98a6d9799259dad7360/3f1cc3cd-2005-4884-839a-244dbc2ab062/woman-researches-genealogy.jpg)

The world of your ancestry is literally at your fingertips! Just start somewhere and go from there. / *Photo by Lokibaho via Getty*

#### Family History Sources Online

There are many sources of info online, in addition to the main genealogy (ancestry, family history) sites. Key types include:

* **newspaper archives** – some of these can be accessed via the main ancestry sites, such as *Newspapers.com* *(Ancestry)*, *OldNews (MyHeritage)*, and *British Newspapers (FindMyPast)*. Plus there’s *Google News Archive*.
* **government and other archives** – the largest worldwide source is the *Internet Archive*
* **cemeteries** – the largest online sites are *FindAGrave* and *BillionGraves*

Of course there are many more avenues of research, depending where your ancestors were from and what their role was in life and society.

#### Learn by Webinar and Video

There are vast family history resources online. These became even more available during the pandemic, and continue to be offered.

Two of the larger sites with how-to videos to help you research your ancestry (family history, genealogy) are:

* *Legacy Family Tree* (Webinars) – there are many videos to watch for free, and some for members-only (paid subscribers)
* *RootsTech* (Family Search annual conference) – videos can be viewed for free during the conference and afterwards

#### Learn from Written Guides

Various genealogy sites have written guides to help you with how-to research and share your family history.

* *FamilySearch Research Wiki* – free online guide with links to genealogy databases, websites, and research strategies to help in the search for your ancestors
* *American Ancestors* – how-to guides on researching and writing about your genealogy
* *The Family History Guide* – free learning resources and activities to help make family history accessible and enjoyable for all ages

#### Regional Groups

There are genealogy groups and family history societies in many locations. Often, local groups are part of a regional or national organization.

Genealogy groups often have a monthly presentation via *Zoom* that you can view at the time, or later as a recorded webinar. You can view worldwide! You can attend in-person if this is an option in your area.

#### DNA Tests and Matches

If you’d like to test your DNA to find out more about your ancestry, these are the main providers. There are other options. It’s best to carefully consider the pros and cons before you do a test, view and share your results.

* *Ancestry DNA* – the largest worldwide database of participants (many from North America)
* *23&Me* – provides health data and info from DNA as well as biological ancestry matches
* *FamilyTree DNA* – offers Y-DNA tests of males, to trace paternal lineage
* *MyHeritage DNA* – most popular DNA test in Europe

#### Professional Genealogists

If you are keen to know your ancestry background, and have the resources, you could hire a professional genealogist. Professionals are accredited by an organization and are to abide by their standards. Their work can help you find ancestors, prove your lineage, and may yield stories and resolve mysteries. They could prepare a genealogy report in a precise standard format.

* The *Association of Professional Genealogists* has a search function on their website to help you [find a genealogist](https://members.apgen.org/members/directory/search_APG.php?org_id=APG) among their members who meets your location and research focus.
* Large ancestry companies – such as *Ancestry*, *MyHeritage* and *American Ancestors* – have genealogists on staff that you can hire.

Or you can find one on your own, in your region or who specializes in the geographic area or culture and language of your ancestors.

### 5) Self-Publishing

Self-publishing is accessible and user-friendly these days. Your product can be digital (e-book or PDF) and/or printed (softcover, hardcover). You only print the number of books you want, and retain control over all the content.

![smiling older woman writes on her laptop while sitting on the couch](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/65b5d98a6d9799259dad7360/b3b9fbd1-a226-4b04-a0fc-1e716646d840/senior-woman-writes-on-laptop.jpg)

Your stories, your way. Go for it! / *Photo by Moe Magners via Pexels*

#### Photo Books

There are many optional services to make a photo book from your pictures, whether about a wedding, vacation, pets, or family history. Two well-known online providers are *MixBook* and *Shutterfly*.

#### Written Books

Two well-known services for self-publishing a book online are *Lulu* and *Blurb*. Major companies like *Amazon* also offer the service. Through any of these, you can offer your book for sale online.

It could be any book, of any length, including photos. You might publish a booklet of your stories and poems, a magazine-length biography about one of your parents, a [family history of any type](/tips/ways-to-tell-family-history), or a memoir.

You may be well-served to work with a self-publishing company that helps you along the way. One well-regarded company in Canada is *Friesen Press*. They provide a set of services including editing, layout and cover.

If you’d like your book to be accepted by a publisher, it will need to be well-written and of interest to the public.

*written by Barbara L Campbell, 2024*

[Barbara Campbell](/?author=6365eabcc01b1722c1a0fef1)

# The Ultimate Guide to Finding the Right App to Record Family Stories

Explore features, tips, and recommendations to make recording and sharing family narratives a seamless and enriching experience. Learn how EarlyBird can help.

By

Jordan Wexler

Last updated:

March 12, 2024

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f9e3058e3ecc6040724fa97/65f0d09f2c5a993e2ff94985_Grandma%20and%20child.png)

10 Minutes

EarlyBird helps parents, family, and friends collectively invest in a child’s financial future. [Learn more.](/)

What You'll Learn

[Why is it So Important to Record Family Stories?](#why-is-it-so-important-to-record-family-stories)

[What to Consider When Looking for the Right App](#what-to-consider-when-looking-for-the-right-app)

[10 Tools to Help You Record Family Stories](#10-tools-to-help-you-record-family-stories)

[Tips for Capturing Your Most Beloved Family Memories](#tips-for-capturing-your-most-beloved-family-memories)

[Make Memory-Sharing Easy With EarlyBird](#make-memory-sharing-easy-with-earlybird)

Recording and passing on family stories has always been a cherished tradition across generations and cultures.

In the digital age, apps have revolutionized the accessibility and [preservation of our most intimate stories](https://www.getearlybird.io/blog/why-saving-memories-is-important-for-your-childs-development).

Whether it's your great-grandmother's immigration tale, your parent's childhood shenanigans, or your own adventures, the right app can make storytelling not only fun and engaging but also everlasting.

From shared family time to bedtime stories, these apps will help you capture it all. Here's how to find the perfect one.

## Why is it So Important to Record Family Stories?

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f9e3058e3ecc6040724fa97/65f0d063f60bccd90e856748_u-Jp8CDmp_z7xlLFUBV8nbfNmnbufLw_Ko0XAAL8peX8mU-OmaDuJUHwjWm5pAEXFJldrMOvWwa7FwPrTJkbDhrWoa1Vf8jHgxwagDOWX6Ml07311q79YR56r7aeUdoZDYRJ06_VunXwDAOvWZieiiA.png)

Family stories aren't simply anecdotes that are fun to listen to. They're a legacy that imparts wisdom, instills values, and preserves history. Through these narratives, children form their identities and develop a sense of belonging and continuity.

According to research, children with a strong family narrative exhibit higher self-esteem, resilience, and a more robust understanding of right and wrong. [Robyn Fivush at Emory University](https://news.emory.edu/stories/2020/04/esc_covid_19_family_stories/campus.html) stated, "When we don’t know what to do, we look for stories about how people have coped in the past. You can see that happening in the media now, in articles comparing today to historical events, like the 1918 flu pandemic and 9/11."

That's just a longer way of saying that family stories build a certain shared capacity for resilience.

The act of recording family stories is not just a pastime. It's truly a gift to your children - and a responsibility.

Family stories carry culture and history into the home. They provide context to world events and personal milestones. They show us that we are woven into a larger, sometimes tumultuous, yet ultimately beautiful saga of humanity.

And the good news is that it no longer has to be a chore to capture these stories. Technology now offers us innovative ways to capture and curate these stories, making it easier and more engaging than writing in a journal or recording on a video camera.

## What to Consider When Looking for the Right App

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f9e3058e3ecc6040724fa97/65f0d063d1c68dcb301db9a6_4stcJE_78Tni0kdXt-aVIgCjGLDezsJu2q5M4fKw3hXcfWORIV9JnlKTV8k0jmQmq08neimdTii5KnHCc3iar0pO7UN2k6Ufk55IhZCr2WNhpzappjePClJJwWhqCiQuD8SplPIcYiLZRt1KWqYi8Xk.png)

Despite how easy they ultimately make things, it can be tough to choose the right app.

Here are a few factors to consider before you start app hunting:

### User-Friendly Interface

An app with a simple and intuitive interface will be more accessible to older family members who may not be tech-savvy. Simplicity in design can encourage broader family participation.

### Privacy and Data Security

Family stories are deeply personal, and their protection should be paramount. Look for apps that offer robust data security measures.

### Sharing Capabilities

Much of the joy of family storytelling comes from sharing these moments with all of your loved ones. The app you choose should make it easy to share content within your family circle, no matter the distance.

### Editing Functions

Speakers may need to correct themselves or tweak their stories. An app with easy editing functions will help maintain the integrity of the narrative without tech-related frustration.

### Longevity and Compatibility

You'll want a long-lasting app. Make sure the one you choose is compatible with various devices and operating systems and has a backup system for data protection. There's no point in choosing an app that will just become obsolete five years from now!

### Value Beyond Recording

Some apps offer additional features that enhance the storytelling experience. Whether it’s multimedia integration or prompts to jog the memory, consider the added value of each app.

## 10 Tools to Help You Record Family Stories

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f9e3058e3ecc6040724fa97/65f0d0639cae4a1fcefe1c8b_Vq7Ct_3Y7lw1oD5QZxNlaNgZI-BcpxbSJ1G2_Sf7wzxkbC2SZfMRoG6woAoezzASLzCX88o6pTd2MDxgMdv1bJat4EFG3vxnBi14t7TLLeENAmgvrJfnHbYNSBLZThMkNgXXuCJFrEyY25Owrl3WaWM.png)

Here are ten apps that stand out for their excellence in recording all your family stories.

### 1. EarlyBird

First on our list is [EarlyBird](https://www.getearlybird.io/): it's more than just a recording app.

EarlyBird is designed specifically for the purpose of family storytelling, offering a unique and compelling user-friendly platform.

The biggest benefit is that it's usable for all age groups, helping to foster the tradition of recording family stories year after year.

The app can record video and audio messages, add images, and even categorize stories by events or individuals.

Here are a few more key features that make EarlyBird a must-have for the modern family historian:

* **Digital Time Capsules**: You can create personalized time capsules for each child, complete with a selection of multimedia stories and milestones. The app ensures these capsules are sealed and can be opened at significant moments in the future — a graduation, wedding, or other life milestones.
* **Secure Sharing**: Stories can be shared within a private family group without the worry of them falling into the wrong hands.
* **Ease of Use**: EarlyBird prides itself on a simple process that makes recording stories accessible to all family members.
* **Life Chapters**: The app organizes stories into life chapters. This creates a ready-made narrative structure for your family's story over time.

### 2. Ancestry.com

Ancestry.com has long been a go-to platform for families interested in their genealogy. However, what many people don't realize is that its functionality extends to story recording for the broader family.

The site also has a feature that helps users uncover and record family stories. It's a deep well of resources with a vast network of users to connect and share memories with.

### 3. Your Smart Phone

The device you carry around every day is a contender for the best app for recording family stories.

With built-in recording capabilities, your phone is a versatile tool for capturing the moments that matter.

### 4. Remento

An app focused on [preserving memories](https://www.getearlybird.io/blog/14-innovative-ways-to-preserve-family-memories), Remento combines AI and human curation to transform buried memories into engaging life stories.

The mission of the app is to revive the art of storytelling, making sure every family member's life events are more than just a fleeting memory.

### 5. Storyworth

StoryWorth prompts family members with weekly questions to jog their memory and encourage the documentation of these personal tales. At the end of the year, the stories are compiled into a beautiful printed book.

### 6. Storied

Storied specializes in high-quality audio recording. It allows users to capture the nuances and inflections of their loved ones' voices, adding another layer of depth to these personal accounts.

### 7. Shutterfly

This website is perhaps best known for its photo book services. However, Shutterfly also offers video story services. These can enhance family narratives through multimedia presentations.

### 8. Animoto

Animoto elevates photo slideshows and videos to professional storytelling levels. It's incredibly easy to use with its drag-and-drop functionality.

### 9. Adobe Spark

Adobe Spark's suite of applications - Spark Post, Spark Page, and Spark Video - allows families to create dynamic stories through graphic design, web pages, and video editing, making it ideal for those with a creative touch.

### 10. StoryCorps App

StoryCorps is a nonprofit that's committed to recording the stories of everyday people.

The app helps users prepare, interview, and record these stories, ultimately contributing to the great library of human narratives.

## Tips for Capturing Your Most Beloved Family Memories

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/5f9e3058e3ecc6040724fa97/65f0d063cebccd96f792a31f_40QADwqc8xq_cjWS61hOWaMrTwY8zdCSnL2EjJU8juc8Eahj53_bcGT3JfkCzIq55w9L4ltV2lp6jzjLOmalBSROXmNN58NyubH-oHsaoOPsLoUDWBkvOfhGXmS4auvu02epdAdbHC7LXFQ9ITN_U_U.png)

Beyond the choice of app, there are a few other tips to keep in mind before you hit "record."

### Prepare Ahead of Time

Never underestimate the power of preparation.

If there are certain stories you're eager to hear from your parents or relatives, jot down the questions that will trigger those memories.

### Think About What You Want to Ask

Craft questions that will elicit descriptive and thoughtful responses rather than one-word answers. Open-ended questions encourage storytelling. They can lead to unexpected tales that might otherwise remain untold.

### Be an Active Listener

Listening is an art. Active listening - even more so.

It can transform a simple recording into a meaningful conversation. Show genuine interest in the stories you're capturing; it will come through in the recording. And don't forget to smile!

### Don't Sweat the Small Stuff

Family recordings don't have to be perfect. The charm lies in the natural dialogue and the joy that comes with shared memories. Technical imperfections can be endearing and should not detract from the essence of the story.

### Keep Your Memories Organized

The more you record, the more important organization becomes. Keep a notebook or digital log of the stories that have been recorded, and use tags or categories within your app to keep everything in order.

Choosing an app that resonates with your family’s values will only enhance the storytelling experience. The right app is the digital key that opens the doors to the treasury of your family's past; what lies behind is an adventure waiting to be shared and enjoyed for years to come.

Whether you start with a voice memo or a full-on video series, the important thing is to get started. The stories you share today will be the legacies that your children and their children will share with their children, and on and on, echoing through time as a testament to family, love, and life.

Make recording family stories an organic part of your family routine, not a chore. Schedule periodic story nights, integrate storytelling into birthdays and holiday gatherings, and most importantly, have fun with it.

Remember, the richest person in the world is the one who has the most vivid and beautiful collection of family stories.

# Best Memoir and Storytelling Services in 2026 (Ranked)

Preserving family stories has never been more important. Whether you want to capture a parent’s life memories, create a legacy gift, or record stories from a grandparent before they’re lost, today’s memoir services make it easier than ever.

But not all storytelling platforms are the same.

Some require writing long answers. Others focus only on video. And only a few truly make it simple for families of all ages to record meaningful stories in a lasting way.

Below are the best memoir and story-recording services in 2026, ranked by ease of use, audio options, keepsake quality, and overall legacy value.

---

## 1. Memorygram (Best Overall for Families and Legacy Keepsakes)

Memorygram is the top memoir and storytelling service available today because it combines everything families actually want in one place: easy story prompts, voice recording, printed legacy books, and even memorial products that can preserve stories forever.

Unlike platforms that rely only on typing, Memorygram makes storytelling accessible for everyone, including seniors, through its phone-based recording option. Storytellers can simply speak naturally, and their audio is saved alongside their memories.

Memorygram also stands out because it offers more than just a book. Families can create Legacy Books, preserve recordings with QR playback, and even connect stories to physical Memory Medallions for remembrance.

**Suitable for:** Families, grandparents, legacy gifts, and anyone who wants audio + print together.

---

## 2. Storyworth

Storyworth is a known memoir services and works well for people who are comfortable writing their stories out each week.

Users receive prompts by email, respond in writing, and at the end of the year, the stories are compiled into a printed book.

While it’s a meaningful product, Storyworth can be challenging for older family members who don’t enjoy typing or who want a more natural way to tell stories out loud.

**Suitable for:** Strong writers who prefer email-based storytelling.

---

## 3. Remento

Remento focuses more heavily on video responses and modern digital sharing. It’s a good option for families who want short video clips and a more casual storytelling format.

However, it may not feel as permanent or keepsake-focused as platforms that prioritize printed books and long-term preservation.

**Suitable for:**Families who prefer video-first memories.

---

## 4. TellMel.ai

TellMel.ai is a newer storytelling platform that uses AI to help guide interviews and generate story structure.

It can be useful for people who want a more automated experience, but it may lack the personal, family-focused keepsake elements that make memoir services truly special.

**Suitable for:**Tech-forward users who want AI assistance.

---

## 5. Meminto

Meminto is another option for recording and organizing life memories, but it is generally more limited compared to platforms that offer full legacy keepsakes.

While it may work for simple digital story collection, it does not provide the same level of family-friendly accessibility, audio preservation, or printed heirloom products that services like Memorygram specialize in.

**Suitable for:** People looking for a basic digital-only memory tool.

---

## Why Memorygram Is the Best Memoir Service in 2026

If your goal is to truly preserve someone’s voice, stories, and legacy in a way that lasts for generations, Memorygram is the strongest option available.

It offers what most competitors do not:

* Record stories by phone instead of typing
* Save real audio forever with QR playback
* Create a printed Legacy Book for the whole family
* Make storytelling easy for seniors and busy families

Memorygram isn’t just a memoir service. It’s a complete legacy platform.

---

## Final Thoughts: Choosing the Right Storytelling Service

The best memoir service depends on what your family needs most.

If you want the easiest, most meaningful way to capture stories in someone’s real voice, and turn them into a lasting keepsake, Memorygram is the clear #1 choice.

Because memories deserve more than text on a screen. They deserve to be heard, held, and passed down.

# Best Memoir Services for Families November 2025

We all mean to ask our parents more about their lives, but somehow, everyday things get in the way. Then one day, we realize there’s so much we still don’t know, like the stories behind old photos, or the moments that shaped them. [Family memoir services](http://storyworth.com) take the pressure off by making memory preservation automatic. Your parents get weekly questions, they share their answers however they want, and at the end of the year, everyone gets a book full of stories that would have otherwise been lost.

**TLDR:**

* Memoir services help busy families preserve parents' stories through weekly prompts and audio recordings.
* Services range from $99 DIY platforms to $3,250+ professional ghostwriting packages.
* Email and phone-based services work better for seniors than app-only platforms.
* Storyworth preserves your parents' own words with unlimited color photos included.

## What Are Memoir Creation Services?

Memoir creation services help families preserve life stories and memories in lasting formats. These services range from [guided question prompts](https://welcome.storyworth.com/blog/60-storyworth-memoirs-question-prompts) that help storytellers write their own narratives to professional ghostwriters who interview your loved one and craft a polished manuscript.

The simplest services send [weekly or monthly prompts](https://welcome.storyworth.com/blog/a-complete-guide-to-storyworths-questions) via email, encouraging storytellers to respond with memories. More involved options include recorded interviews, transcription services, and professional editing. Some handle the entire process from initial conversation to final book production.

For busy adults juggling careers and families, these services solve a common challenge: [how to preserve parents' stories](https://welcome.storyworth.com/blog/honor-a-loved-one-with-a-memorial-book) without adding another time-consuming project to an already packed schedule. They provide structure and accountability that make memory preservation a reality.

## How We Ranked Memoir Creation Services

We looked at each service based on five criteria that matter most when preserving a parent's stories while managing your own busy life.

* **Ease of use for seniors**. Can your parent participate without downloading apps or learning complicated software? We picked services that work through familiar methods, such as email or phone calls, instead of requiring technical fluency.
* **Pricing transparency**. We looked for clear, upfront pricing with no hidden fees. Services that include the book cost in the initial purchase ranked higher than those with unclear add-on charges.
* **Book quality**. The final product matters. We assessed cover durability, print quality, page limits, and whether color photos cost extra.
* **Customer support.** When questions arise, you need help. We looked at availability and response channels.
* **Family collaboration.** Can multiple family members participate, suggest questions, or receive stories as they're written? We favored services that make memory preservation a shared family experience.

## Best Overall Memoir Creation Service: Storyworth

Storyworth sends weekly email prompts to your parent, who can respond by replying to the email, typing on the website, or recording a voice message on a smartphone or landline. We transcribe those phone recordings automatically. Once they share their story, they can go back and edit or add to it at any time.

We preserve your parents' original words and phrasing instead of rewriting responses with AI. You can upload unlimited photos in full color at no extra charge. The included hardcover book holds up to 480 pages, printed on archival-quality materials.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/615f0d36e3e97b470b88fa0e/691ae1a60f8b528c0ea669d5_FkzcsVhw_5sj7NcaYoIHg.png)

Family members can receive stories as they're written, comment on them, add new questions, and collaborate on questions together.

Since 2013, we've published over 1 million books and helped families share 35 million stories.

## Remento

Remento focuses on voice and video recording versus written responses. Storytellers receive weekly prompts and record their answers through the Remento app, which uses AI to transcribe recordings into text.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/615f0d36e3e97b470b88fa0e/691ae1a60f8b528c0ea669de_w0hg-JOHIBCzjj5kLx0L4.png)

Their distinctive feature is embedding QR codes in printed books that link to original audio or video recordings. This lets readers scan and hear stories in the storyteller's actual voice, combining written and audio formats. Family members can collaborate on selecting prompts, and full-color printing comes standard.

The service's AI may rewrite transcribed responses to improve readability, potentially changing your parents' original phrasing and authentic voice. Remento suits families who are comfortable with app-based recording and AI editing, although voice authenticity may be compromised.

## StoryTerrace

StoryTerrace assigns professional ghostwriters who interview your parents and write their memoirs for them. The service offers multiple tiers based on writer experience, from less experienced authors to executive-level writers with publishing industry backgrounds.

Writers conduct extensive interviews with the storytellers, then craft a polished manuscript on their behalf. Packages include professional editing, hardcover book production, and optional publishing consultation.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/615f0d36e3e97b470b88fa0e/691ae1a60f8b528c0ea669db__CPjwxKC7Dk6i-MLeVxat.png)

Packages start at $3,250, placing StoryTerrace in the luxury memoir category. This pricing reflects the professional ghostwriting service versus a guided self-writing approach.

For families seeking an accessible way to capture stories in their parents' own voice, the price point makes it impractical. StoryTerrace works well if you want a professionally written memoir and budget isn't a concern.

## Meminto

Meminto is a memoir service that offers themed memory books and mobile-app recording with QR code integration for multimedia content.

Meminto offers:

* Themed books covering travel, weddings, and memorials beyond traditional life stories
* Mobile app with speech-to-text recording
* QR codes that link to audio and video content in printed books
* One-time purchase without ongoing subscriptions

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/615f0d36e3e97b470b88fa0e/691ae1a60f8b528c0ea669d0_GWUSrR8WUDZKVM08q1pF6.png)

The app-first design requires smartphone comfort, which can be a barrier for less tech-savvy parents and eldery. And while themed options set Meminto apart from other memoir creation services, the multi-product focus might introduce more complexity.

Meminto suits families who want themed memory books and feel comfortable with app-based recording. For life story preservation with reliable support, [services with longer track records](https://welcome.storyworth.com/blog/storyworth-for-fathers-day-real-reviews-from-families) may serve you better.

## My Life in a Book

My Life in a Book provides guided memoir creation through structured questionnaire formats. Storytellers work through interview-style questions, which the service then designs into a professionally laid-out hardcover book.

My Life in a Book offers:

* Structured questionnaire approach to story collection
* Professional book design and layout services
* Photo integration throughout the narrative
* Additional copy ordering for family distribution

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/615f0d36e3e97b470b88fa0e/691ad5eefd80f1a9a816d616_zs1K1__uRU5ZAvp_5Tvbr.png)

The service requires storytellers to complete extensive questionnaires, which demand focus and time commitment. For seniors who tire easily or have busy schedules, this format can feel overwhelming compared to weekly email prompts that break the process into manageable pieces.

My Life in a Book works well if your parents enjoy structured formats and have dedicated time for memoir writing. For families seeking a less intensive approach that accommodates varying energy levels, services with flexible weekly prompts may prove more realistic.

## Feature Comparison Table of Memoir Creation Services

Here's how the major memoir creation services stack up on features that matter most to busy families:

| Feature | Storyworth | Remento | StoryTerrace | Meminto | My Life in a Book |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Starting Price | $99 | $99 | $3,250 for a classic book | $99 for a book up to 100 pages | $79 for a one-year Story Prompt Program, which includes one full-color keepsake book |
| Story Submission | Email, web, phone call | Audio/video recordings | Written/voice-to-text | Mobile app | Written answers; limited voice options |
| Photos per Story | Unlimited | Unlimited | Varies by package | 250 | Varies |
| Color Printing | Included | Included | Included | Included | Included |
| Max Pages Included | 480 | 250 | Varies | Up to 100 pages | Varies |
| Family Collaboration | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | No |
| Support Availability | 24/7 email/text, phone hours | Email | Email | Email | Email |
| Book Format | 6"x9" hardcover | 8" x 10" hardcover | Custom | 8.5x8.5" | 6" x 8" hardcover |

‍

Each service takes a different approach to memory preservation, but accessibility and collaboration set some apart for families managing busy schedules.

## Why Storyworth is the Best Memoir Creation Service

The [genealogy and family history market continues to grow](https://www.polarismarketresearch.com/industry-analysis/genealogy-products-and-services-market) as more families recognize the importance of documenting stories for future generations. At Storyworth, we've spent over a decade meeting this need through accessible, authentic memoir creation.

Our track record speaks clearly: over 1 million books printed and nearly [50,000 five-star reviews](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/storyworth.com) on Trustpilot. This success comes from keeping memoir creation simple enough for any parent to participate, regardless of tech comfort level.

Being family-owned means we answer to families, not investors. Your parents' stories remain private.

Unlike services that use AI to rewrite responses or require app downloads, we preserve your parents' authentic voice through methods they already know. For busy adults who want their parents' stories preserved without adding complexity to anyone's life, Storyworth removes every barrier between intention and completion.

## Final thoughts on memoir creation services for your family

Capturing your parents' memories shouldn't feel like another project on your to-do list. The right [memoir creation service](http://storyworth.com) removes the barriers between wanting to preserve stories and actually getting them written. Choose something simple, start this week, and watch a year of weekly stories become the family heirloom you'll treasure most.

## FAQ

### How do memoir creation services work for parents who aren't comfortable with technology?

The best services allow your parents to respond through familiar methods like email replies or phone calls instead of requiring apps or logins. At Storyworth, your parents can reply to weekly email prompts or record their answer over a phone call, which we automatically transcribe into text.

### What's included in the cost of a memoir creation service?

Most services include weekly prompts, story collection, and one printed hardcover book. Storyworth includes unlimited color photos at no extra charge, up to 480 pages in your book, and 24/7 customer support for $99, with no hidden fees.

### Can family members help with the memoir creation process?

Some services allow family collaboration while others don't. With Storyworth, multiple family members can suggest questions, receive stories as they're written, and reply with comments, as well as help edit the final book together, making memory preservation a shared family project.

### How long does it take to create a complete memoir book?

Most memoir services work on a one-year timeline, sending weekly or monthly prompts that break the process into manageable pieces. This pacing prevents overwhelm and gives your parent time to reflect on each memory without feeling rushed.

### What happens to my parents' stories and personal information?

Privacy policies vary by service. At Storyworth, we're family-owned with no outside investors or advertisers, so your parents' stories remain completely private and are never used for marketing or shared externally, and only invited family members can view them.

# Best Life Story Books and Memory Books in 2026

February 13, 2026 · Updated February 13, 2026

  ![Best Life Story Books and Memory Books in 2026](/images/1734d94ff5-P5-featured-004.jpg)

*Your mum’s childhood home. Your dad’s first job. The story of how your grandparents met. These memories exist in only one place – in the heads of the people who lived them. A life story book captures them before they fade.*

*But “life story book” means different things to different people. Some are fill-in-the-blank journals you buy on Amazon. Others are custom biographies built from recorded conversations. Some are simple question-and-answer keepsakes. Others are full narratives with photos, timelines, and decades of family history.*

*This guide covers all of them – from blank books to digital services – so you can find the format that works for your family.*

---

## What is a Life Story Book?

A life story book is any book that captures someone’s personal history – their childhood, relationships, career, lessons, and memories – in a format that can be read, shared, and kept by their family.

They range from:

* **Blank journals** with guided questions (you buy the book, your parent fills it in)
* **Fill-in-the-blank memory books** (structured prompts, short written answers)
* **Interview-based biographies** (you record conversations, then turn them into a book)
* **AI-assisted life story services** (apps that help capture, write, and print the biography)
* **Professional memoir writing services** (a ghostwriter interviews your parent and writes the book)

The right choice depends on your parent’s personality, your budget, and how involved you want to be in the process.

---

## Blank Journals and Fill-In Books

These are the simplest option: buy a physical book, hand it to your parent, and they fill in the answers. Available on Amazon, in bookshops, and from gift retailers.

### Popular Options

**My Life Story** – A structured journal with prompts covering childhood, family, career, and reflections. Clean layout with space for photos. One of the bestselling life story books in the UK.

**Grandparent’s Story** and **Mum’s Story** – Part of a series of family-specific journals with questions tailored to the relationship. Good as gifts because the cover makes the intent clear.

**Your Life in Your Words** – A fill-in journal with broader, more open-ended questions. Works for any family member.

### Best For

Parents who enjoy writing and are happy working through a book on their own. These make lovely gifts – especially for Mother’s Day, Father’s Day, and Christmas – because the physical book is the gift itself.

### Limitations

Your parent has to do all the work. That sounds obvious, but it’s the reason many life story books end up half-finished on a shelf. Writing doesn’t come naturally to everyone. Some people find it easier to talk about their memories than to write them down. And the fill-in format limits stories to what fits in the space provided – some of the best family stories need more room than a few lines on a page.

### Price

Typically between £10-25 for a quality journal.

---

## Question-Based Digital Services

These services send your parent regular questions and compile their written responses into a book.

### StoryWorth

The best-known name in this space. StoryWorth emails your parent a question each week for a year. They write their response (as long or short as they like), and at the end, the answers are compiled into a hardcover book.

* **Price:** $99/year (approximately £80), includes one hardcover book
* **Best for:** Parents who enjoy writing and are comfortable working on a computer or phone independently
* **Format:** Written Q&A compiled into a physical book
* **Limitation:** Solo experience – your parent works alone. Voice recording is available but the experience is primarily built around writing

For a detailed comparison of StoryWorth and its alternatives, see our [complete review of StoryWorth alternatives](/blog/best-storyworth-alternatives-2026).

### Remento

Similar concept to StoryWorth, but voice-first. Your parent receives prompts and records spoken answers. Remento’s AI converts the audio into written narrative.

* **Price:** $99/year (approximately £80)
* **Best for:** Parents who prefer talking to typing
* **Format:** Voice recordings turned into written biography via AI
* **Limitation:** Still a solo experience – your parent responds alone

---

## Conversation-Based Life Story Services

These tools are built around two people sitting together – you and your parent – rather than your parent working alone.

### Willow

*Disclosure: This is our product.*

Willow provides guided conversation prompts designed for you and your parent to use together. You choose a topic, Willow suggests questions, and you record the conversation as it unfolds naturally. Willow’s AI then turns your recorded conversations into a written biography.

The difference from fill-in books and prompt services is the **together experience**. You’re there for the stories. You ask follow-up questions. You hear things you never knew. The conversation captures details that a fill-in book or solo prompt never would.

* **Price:** Free 14-day trial (no credit card required), paid plans after
* **Best for:** Adult children who want to sit with a parent and capture their story together
* **Format:** Recorded conversations turned into written biography via AI
* **How it works:** Choose a topic, record your conversation, Willow writes it up

If you’re already planning to interview your parent, our guide on [how to interview your parents about their life](/blog/how-to-interview-your-parents-about-their-life) covers the practical side.

### StoryCorps

A free, non-profit app for recording conversations between two people. Great for capturing raw audio of your parent’s stories. Recordings can be archived at the Library of Congress.

* **Price:** Free
* **Best for:** Families who want to preserve the audio itself – the sound of a parent’s voice
* **Format:** Audio recordings only (no writing, no book printing)
* **Limitation:** Recording tool only – no transcription, no written output, no book

---

## Professional Memoir Writing Services

If budget allows, you can hire a professional to interview your parent and write their biography. These services typically cost hundreds or thousands of pounds, but the result is a polished, professionally written book.

### What to Expect

A ghostwriter or memoir service will:

1. Interview your parent over several sessions (usually 5-10 hours of conversation)
2. Transcribe and organise the material
3. Write a full narrative biography (typically 20,000-60,000 words)
4. Edit and revise based on family feedback
5. Help with printing or publishing

### Price

Professional memoir services in the UK typically start from around £2,000 for a basic written biography and can reach £5,000-10,000+ for a full-length, professionally edited and printed book.

### Best For

Families who want a high-quality, professionally written biography and have the budget for it. Particularly popular for milestone birthdays (70th, 80th, 90th) where the family wants to create something special.

---

## The DIY Approach

You don’t need any product or service to create a life story book. With a phone recorder, a word processor, and some time, you can do everything yourself.

**Record:** Use your phone’s built-in recorder. Otter.ai offers AI transcription (free plan available with limited imports; paid plan for more).

**Write:** Google Docs or any word processor. Our guide on [how to write a family biography](/blog/how-to-write-a-family-biography) walks through the full process.

**Print:** Blurb and Mixbook offer affordable photo book printing with flexible layouts. Local print shops can produce beautiful hardcover books.

**The trade-off:** Complete control and minimal cost, but significant time investment. Transcribing, organising, writing, editing, and formatting a biography from scratch takes many hours. For some families, that hands-on process is part of the gift. For others, it’s the reason the project stalls.

---

## Comparison Table

| Type | Cost | Your Parent Works Alone? | Written Output | Physical Book | Best For |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Fill-in journals** | £10-25 | Yes | Short answers | The journal itself | Gift-givers, parents who enjoy writing |
| **StoryWorth** | ~£80/year | Yes | Compiled Q&A | Included | Independent writers |
| **Remento** | ~£80/year | Yes | AI-written from voice | Available | Parents who prefer talking |
| **Willow** | Free trial | No – you do it together | AI-written from conversation | Coming soon | Families who want the shared experience |
| **StoryCorps** | Free | No – conversation | Audio only | No | Preserving voices |
| **Professional memoir** | £2,000-10,000+ | No – interviewer present | Professionally written | Yes | Milestone gifts, high-quality finish |
| **DIY** | Free-low | Up to you | You write it | Via Blurb etc. | Hands-on families |

---

## How to Choose

### Start with Your Parent’s Personality

This matters more than anything else. The best life story book is the one that actually gets finished.

* **Loves writing:** Fill-in journal or StoryWorth
* **Prefers talking:** Remento or Willow
* **Lights up in conversation with you:** Willow or StoryCorps
* **Would never start on their own:** You need to be involved – choose a conversation-based option or hire a professional

### Consider What You Want at the End

* **A physical book to hold:** Fill-in journal, StoryWorth, Remento, professional service, or DIY with Blurb
* **A written biography to read:** Willow, Remento, StoryWorth, professional service, or DIY
* **Audio of their voice:** StoryCorps, Willow, or your phone recorder
* **A quick, easy gift:** Fill-in journal (ready to wrap today)

### Think About Budget

* **Free:** StoryCorps, DIY
* **Under £25:** Fill-in journal
* **Under £100/year:** StoryWorth, Remento, Willow
* **£2,000+:** Professional memoir service

### Think About Your Involvement

* **Hands-off (gift and forget):** Fill-in journal, StoryWorth
* **Light involvement:** Remento (you set it up, they do the rest)
* **Active participant:** Willow, StoryCorps, DIY
* **None (outsource it):** Professional memoir service

---

## Life Story Books as Gifts

Life story books are among the most popular gifts for Mother’s Day, Father’s Day, and Christmas. They work because the gift is the experience of capturing stories, not just a product.

A few tips for giving a life story book as a gift:

* **Fill-in journals** are the easiest to wrap and give. They work on their own.
* **Subscriptions** (StoryWorth, Remento, Willow) are experience gifts – print a card explaining what you’ve set up for them.
* **The best gift is your time.** If you’re giving a conversation-based option, the real gift is the hours you’ll spend together. Make that clear.

For questions to get the conversation started, see our [50 questions to ask your grandparents](/blog/50-questions-to-ask-your-grandparents-about-their-life).

---

## The Most Important Thing

A half-finished journal is better than no journal. A single recorded conversation is better than the perfect biography you never start. A phone recording of your mum telling one story at the kitchen table is something your grandchildren will treasure.

Pick whatever feels right. Start small. The stories are what matter – the format is just how you hold onto them.
